# vike-sitemap-generator

> Vite plugin for Vike that automatically generates sitemap.xml at build time

vike-sitemap-generator is a Vite build plugin for Vike (a full-stack framework built on Vite). It collects page URLs from Vike's prerender context (SSG) or route config (SSR) and generates standards-compliant sitemap XML during the build.

## Key features

- Automatic URL collection from Vike's SSG prerender context or SSR route patterns
- Per-page metadata via co-located `+sitemap.ts` files using Vike's extension system
- Per-page URL enumeration via `+sitemapUrls.ts` for SSR apps with parameterized routes
- Parallel metadata resolution with configurable concurrency (default 10)
- Automatic sitemap index splitting for large sites exceeding 50,000 URLs
- External sitemap references for multi-app domains (sitemap index)
- Configurable `<lastmod>`, `<priority>`, `<changefreq>`, and `<image:image>` per URL
- Trailing slash control (boolean, per-route rules, or function)
- Exclude paths by exact string, regex, or per-page `exclude: true`
- Optional `robots.txt` integration (auto-append `Sitemap:` directive)
- Dry-run mode for previewing output without writing files
- Configurable output directory independent of Vite's build output
- Built-in `getLastModFromGit` and `getLastModFromGithub` helpers

## Install

```
npm install vike-sitemap-generator
# or
bun add vike-sitemap-generator
# or
pnpm add vike-sitemap-generator
```

Peer dependencies: `vike >= 0.4.0`, `vite >= 7.0.0`

## Export paths

- `vike-sitemap-generator` -- main plugin export (`vikeSitemap`), type exports, and fetcher helpers (`getLastModFromGit`, `getLastModFromGithub`)
- `vike-sitemap-generator/config` -- Vike extension config (default export) for `+sitemap.ts` and `+sitemapUrls.ts` support

## Quick start

Add the plugin to your Vite config:

```ts
// vite.config.ts
import vike from "vike/plugin";
import { vikeSitemap } from "vike-sitemap-generator";

export default {
  plugins: [
    vike(),
    vikeSitemap({
      baseUrl: "https://example.com",
    }),
  ],
};
```

This generates a `sitemap.xml` in your build output directory after the client build completes.

## Plugin options reference

All options are passed to `vikeSitemap()` in your Vite config.

### `baseUrl` (required)

The base URL of your site. Must not end with a trailing slash.

```ts
vikeSitemap({ baseUrl: "https://example.com" });
```

### `outFile`

Path for the generated sitemap, relative to the output directory. Defaults to `"sitemap.xml"`.

```ts
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "https://example.com",
  outFile: "custom/path/sitemap.xml",
});
```

### `outDir`

Output directory independent of Vite's `build.outDir`. Accepts an absolute path or a path relative to your project root. When omitted, falls back to the client build's output directory.

```ts
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "https://example.com",
  outDir: "dist/public",
});
```

Relative paths are resolved against Vite's project root. Absolute paths are used as-is. Nested directories are created automatically.

### `trailingSlash`

Configure trailing slashes on URLs in the sitemap. Accepts a boolean, an array of rules (first match wins), or a function.

```ts
// Add trailing slash to all URLs
vikeSitemap({ baseUrl: "...", trailingSlash: true });

// Remove trailing slash from all URLs
vikeSitemap({ baseUrl: "...", trailingSlash: false });

// Per-route rules (exact strings or RegExp)
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "...",
  trailingSlash: [
    { match: /^\/blog/, trailingSlash: true },
    { match: /^\/docs/, trailingSlash: false },
  ],
});

// Function with access to all URLs
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "...",
  trailingSlash: (url, { urls }) => {
    return urls.some((u) => u !== url && u.startsWith(`${url}/`));
  },
});
```

The root URL `/` is never modified. Per-route rules are evaluated in order; the first matching rule wins. Unmatched URLs are left unchanged. The trailing slash transformation runs before metadata resolution; per-page `+sitemap.ts` files do not control trailing slashes.

### `lastmod`

Async callback to resolve `<lastmod>` for each URL. Receives the URL path (e.g. `"/about"`) and should return an ISO 8601 date string, or `undefined` to omit.

```ts
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "...",
  lastmod: async (url) => {
    const res = await fetch(`https://cms.example.com/meta?path=${url}`);
    const data = await res.json();
    return data.updatedAt;
  },
});
```

### `priority`

Configure `<priority>` per route. Accepts a number (applied to all), an array of rules (first match wins), or a function.

```ts
// Uniform priority
vikeSitemap({ baseUrl: "...", priority: 0.5 });

// Per-route rules
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "...",
  priority: [
    { match: "/", priority: 1.0 },
    { match: /^\/blog/, priority: 0.8 },
  ],
});

// Function
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "...",
  priority: (url, { urls }) => {
    const hasChildren = urls.some(
      (u) => u !== url && u.startsWith(`${url === "/" ? "" : url}/`),
    );
    return hasChildren ? 0.8 : 0.5;
  },
});
```

### `changefreq`

Configure `<changefreq>` per route. Accepts a single value or an array of rules.

Valid values: `"always"`, `"hourly"`, `"daily"`, `"weekly"`, `"monthly"`, `"yearly"`, `"never"`.

```ts
vikeSitemap({ baseUrl: "...", changefreq: "weekly" });

// Per-route rules
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "...",
  changefreq: [
    { match: "/", changefreq: "daily" },
    { match: /^\/blog/, changefreq: "weekly" },
    { match: /^\/docs/, changefreq: "monthly" },
  ],
});
```

### `images`

Async callback to resolve `<image:image>` entries for each URL (Google Image Sitemap extension). Receives the URL path and should return an array of `SitemapImage` objects, or `undefined` to omit.

```ts
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "...",
  images: async (url) => {
    const res = await fetch(`https://cms.example.com/images?path=${url}`);
    const data = await res.json();
    return data.images.map((img) => ({
      loc: img.url,
      title: img.alt,
    }));
  },
});
```

SitemapImage fields:
- `loc` (string, required) -- URL of the image
- `caption` (string, optional) -- Caption or description
- `geoLocation` (string, optional) -- Geographic location (e.g. "New York, NY")
- `title` (string, optional) -- Title of the image
- `license` (string, optional) -- URL to the license

When any entry has images, the `image` XML namespace is automatically added to the `<urlset>`.

### `additionalUrls`

Include URLs that Vike can't discover automatically, such as SSR apps with parameterized routes that aren't prerendered. Additional URLs are matched against page route patterns, so per-page `+sitemap.ts` config and route params are still resolved for these URLs.

```ts
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "...",
  additionalUrls: ["/product/1", "/product/2", "/product/3"],
});
```

### `exclude`

An array of paths to exclude from the sitemap. Each entry can be an exact path string or a RegExp pattern.

```ts
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "...",
  exclude: [
    "/admin",
    /^\/internal/,
  ],
});
```

String entries use exact matching. Use a RegExp like `/^\/admin/` to exclude an entire subtree.

### `concurrency`

Maximum number of concurrent metadata resolutions. Defaults to `10`. Set to `Infinity` for unlimited parallelism.

When `lastmod` or `images` callbacks involve async I/O (git commands, API calls, CMS fetches), this controls how many run in parallel. URLs are processed in batches; within each batch, all async callbacks run concurrently via `Promise.all`.

```ts
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "...",
  lastmod: async (url) => { /* ... */ },
  concurrency: 20,
});
```

Guidelines:
- External API calls (CMS, GitHub): Use `5` or `10` to stay within rate limits.
- Local git commands: `10-20` is a reasonable range.
- In-memory lookups: Use `Infinity` for maximum throughput.

Per-page `+sitemap.ts` values bypass the global callbacks entirely, freeing up concurrency slots.

### `maxUrlsPerSitemap`

Maximum URLs per sitemap file. When exceeded, the plugin automatically splits into multiple numbered sitemap files and generates a sitemap index. Defaults to `50000` (the sitemap protocol limit).

```ts
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "...",
  maxUrlsPerSitemap: 10000,
});
```

This produces `sitemap-0.xml`, `sitemap-1.xml`, ..., and a `sitemap.xml` index that references them.

Example index XML:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<sitemapindex xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">
  <sitemap>
    <loc>https://example.com/sitemap-0.xml</loc>
  </sitemap>
  <sitemap>
    <loc>https://example.com/sitemap-1.xml</loc>
  </sitemap>
</sitemapindex>
```

### `robots`

When enabled, appends a `Sitemap:` directive to `robots.txt` in the output directory, pointing to the generated sitemap. Creates the file if it doesn't exist. Avoids duplicate entries. Defaults to `false`.

```ts
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "https://example.com",
  robots: true,
});
```

Behavior:
- If `robots.txt` exists, appends `Sitemap: https://example.com/sitemap.xml` at the end (unless already present).
- If `robots.txt` does not exist, creates it with just the directive.
- Running the build multiple times does not create duplicate entries.
- When using a sitemap index, the directive points to the index file, not individual numbered files.

### `dryRun`

When enabled, logs the generated XML to the console without writing any files. Useful for debugging. Defaults to `false`.

```ts
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "https://example.com",
  dryRun: true,
});
```

In dry-run mode:
- No `sitemap.xml` or split sitemap files are written.
- No `robots.txt` is written (even if `robots: true`).
- All metadata resolution (lastmod, images, etc.) still runs normally so you can verify the full pipeline.

### `externalSitemaps`

An array of external sitemap URLs to include in the generated sitemap index. When provided, the plugin always produces a sitemap index that references both the locally generated sitemap(s) and these external URLs. This is useful when your domain hosts multiple independent sites that each generate their own sitemap (e.g. a docs site at `/docs`).

```ts
vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "https://example.com",
  externalSitemaps: [
    "https://example.com/docs/sitemap.xml",
    "https://example.com/blog/sitemap.xml",
  ],
});
```

Behavior:
- When `externalSitemaps` is non-empty, the plugin always generates a sitemap index, even if the local URL count is below `maxUrlsPerSitemap`.
- Local URLs are written to `sitemap-0.xml` (or chunked into `sitemap-0.xml`, `sitemap-1.xml`, ... if they exceed `maxUrlsPerSitemap`).
- The sitemap index file (at `outFile`, default `sitemap.xml`) references both the local sitemap file(s) and the external URLs.
- External URLs are appended after local sitemaps in the index.
- When omitted or empty, behavior is identical to previous versions (no sitemap index for small sites).

## Per-page metadata with `+sitemap.ts`

Co-locate sitemap metadata alongside your pages using Vike's extension system. Per-page values take precedence over global plugin options.

### Setup

Register the Vike extension in your root page config:

```ts
// pages/+config.ts
import vikeSitemapConfig from "vike-sitemap-generator/config";

export default {
  extends: [vikeSitemapConfig],
};
```

This registers `sitemap` as a recognized Vike setting, enabling `+sitemap.ts` files throughout your page tree.

### Defining per-page metadata

Create a `+sitemap.ts` (or `+sitemap.js`) file in any page directory:

```ts
// pages/about/+sitemap.ts
import type { SitemapPageConfig } from "vike-sitemap-generator";

export default {
  priority: 0.8,
  changefreq: "monthly",
  lastmod: "2025-06-15",
} satisfies SitemapPageConfig;
```

### SitemapPageConfig fields

- `priority` (number) -- `<priority>` value between 0.0 and 1.0
- `changefreq` (Changefreq) -- `<changefreq>` value (e.g. `"weekly"`)
- `lastmod` (string) -- `<lastmod>` ISO 8601 date string
- `images` (SitemapImage[]) -- Array of image objects for `<image:image>`
- `exclude` (boolean) -- When `true`, excludes the page from the sitemap

### Dynamic routes with callbacks

For dynamic routes (e.g. `/blog/@slug`), export a function instead of a static object. The function receives a `SitemapPageContext` with the concrete URL, extracted route parameters, and the page's `data` from `+data.ts`:

```ts
// pages/blog/@slug/+data.ts
export type Data = { title: string; updatedAt: string; images: { url: string; alt: string }[] };
export default async function (pageContext) {
  const post = await fetchPost(pageContext.routeParams.slug);
  return post;
}

// pages/blog/@slug/+sitemap.ts
import type { SitemapPageConfigFn } from "vike-sitemap-generator";
import type { Data } from "./+data";

export default ((context) => ({
  priority: 0.7,
  changefreq: "weekly",
  lastmod: context.data.updatedAt,
  images: context.data.images.map((img) => ({ loc: img.url, title: img.alt })),
})) satisfies SitemapPageConfigFn<Data>;
```

The callback runs after `+data.ts` has been resolved during prerendering, so `context.data` contains the same data your page component receives.

The callback can also be async:

```ts
// pages/product/@id/+sitemap.ts
import type { SitemapPageConfigFn } from "vike-sitemap-generator";

export default (async (context) => {
  return {
    priority: context.data.featured ? 1.0 : 0.5,
    lastmod: context.data.updatedAt,
    images: context.data.images.map((img) => ({ loc: img.url, title: img.alt })),
  };
}) satisfies SitemapPageConfigFn;
```

SitemapPageContext fields:
- `url` (string) -- The concrete URL path (e.g. `"/blog/post-1"`)
- `routeParams` (Record<string, string>) -- Extracted route params (e.g. `{ slug: "post-1" }`)
- `data` (Data) -- Page data from `+data.ts`, available during SSG prerendering. Typed as `unknown` by default; pass your `Data` type as a generic parameter to `SitemapPageConfigFn<Data>` for type safety.

SitemapPageConfigFn type:
```ts
type SitemapPageConfigFn<Data = unknown> = (context: SitemapPageContext<Data>) => SitemapPageConfig | Promise<SitemapPageConfig>;
```

### Excluding pages

```ts
// pages/admin/+sitemap.ts
export default {
  exclude: true,
};
```

### Precedence rules

Per-page values always override global options:

- `+sitemap.ts` `priority: 0.9` overrides global `priority: 0.5` for that page.
- `+sitemap.ts` `lastmod: "2024-01-01"` overrides the global `lastmod` callback result.
- `+sitemap.ts` `images` prevents the global `images` callback from being called for that URL.
- Fields not set in `+sitemap.ts` fall through to the global callbacks/rules.
- Function configs follow the same precedence: returned fields override globals, unset fields fall through.

### Per-page images

```ts
// pages/gallery/+sitemap.ts
export default {
  images: [
    {
      loc: "https://example.com/photos/sunset.jpg",
      title: "Sunset over the ocean",
      caption: "A beautiful sunset photographed at Malibu Beach",
    },
    {
      loc: "https://example.com/photos/mountain.jpg",
      title: "Mountain landscape",
    },
  ],
};
```

### URL enumeration with `+sitemapUrls.ts`

For SSR apps with parameterized routes (e.g. `/blog/@slug`), the plugin cannot discover concrete URLs automatically since there is no prerender context. You can provide a `+sitemapUrls.ts` file alongside your page to enumerate the URLs that should appear in the sitemap.

The `+sitemapUrls.ts` file exports either a static array of URL paths or a function (sync or async) that returns them:

```ts
// pages/blog/@slug/+sitemapUrls.ts (static list)
import type { SitemapUrlsConfig } from "vike-sitemap-generator";

export default [
  "/blog/hello-world",
  "/blog/second-post",
] satisfies SitemapUrlsConfig;
```

```ts
// pages/blog/@slug/+sitemapUrls.ts (async function)
import type { SitemapUrlsConfig } from "vike-sitemap-generator";

export default (async () => {
  const posts = await fetch("https://cms.example.com/posts").then((r) => r.json());
  return posts.map((post) => `/blog/${post.slug}`);
}) satisfies SitemapUrlsConfig;
```

URLs from `+sitemapUrls.ts` are matched against page route patterns, so per-page `+sitemap.ts` config and route params are still resolved for them.

This requires the Vike extension to be registered in `pages/+config.ts` (same setup as `+sitemap.ts`).

SitemapUrlsConfig type:
```ts
type SitemapUrlsConfig = string[] | (() => string[] | Promise<string[]>);
```

### Combining `+sitemapUrls.ts` with `+sitemap.ts`

You can use both files together. `+sitemapUrls.ts` provides the list of concrete URLs, and `+sitemap.ts` provides per-page metadata for each of those URLs:

```ts
// pages/blog/@slug/+sitemapUrls.ts
export default ["/blog/hello-world", "/blog/second-post"];

// pages/blog/@slug/+sitemap.ts
import type { SitemapPageConfigFn } from "vike-sitemap-generator";
import type { Data } from "./+data";

export default ((context) => ({
  priority: 0.7,
  changefreq: "weekly",
  lastmod: context.data.updatedAt,
})) satisfies SitemapPageConfigFn<Data>;
```

### SSG vs SSR behavior

- SSG (prerendering): The plugin matches prerendered URLs back to their page route patterns to attach per-page metadata and extract route params. Both static and parameterized routes are matched correctly.
- SSR (no prerendering): The plugin reads `page.config.sitemap` directly from each static page entry in Vike's config. Parameterized routes (containing `@`) are excluded unless their concrete URLs are provided via `additionalUrls` or `+sitemapUrls.ts`.
- `+sitemapUrls.ts`: URLs enumerated by `+sitemapUrls.ts` files are collected from all pages and matched against route patterns for per-page config resolution.
- Additional URLs (`additionalUrls`): matched against page route patterns so that per-page `+sitemap.ts` config and route params are resolved.

## Fetchers

Built-in helpers for resolving `<lastmod>` from git history.

### `getLastModFromGit` (local)

Uses the local git binary to get the last commit date for a file.

```ts
import { getLastModFromGit, vikeSitemap } from "vike-sitemap-generator";

vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "https://example.com",
  lastmod: async (url) => {
    const filePath =
      url === "/" ? "pages/index/+Page.tsx" : `pages${url}/+Page.tsx`;
    return getLastModFromGit({ filePath });
  },
});
```

Options:
- `filePath` (string, required) -- Path to the file, relative to `cwd`
- `cwd` (string, optional) -- Working directory for the git command. Defaults to `process.cwd()`

Returns the date of the most recent commit as `YYYY-MM-DD`, or `undefined` if the file has no git history, does not exist, or git is not available.

### `getLastModFromGithub` (remote)

Fetches the last commit date via the GitHub REST API. Useful when your build environment doesn't have git history available (e.g. shallow clones in CI).

```ts
import { getLastModFromGithub, vikeSitemap } from "vike-sitemap-generator";

vikeSitemap({
  baseUrl: "https://example.com",
  lastmod: async (url) => {
    const filePath =
      url === "/" ? "pages/index/+Page.tsx" : `pages${url}/+Page.tsx`;
    return getLastModFromGithub({
      token: process.env.GH_TOKEN!,
      repo: "owner/repo",
      filePath,
    });
  },
});
```

Options:
- `token` (string, required) -- GitHub personal access token with repo read access
- `repo` (string, required) -- Repository in `owner/repo` format
- `filePath` (string, required) -- Path to the file in the repository

Returns the date of the most recent commit as `YYYY-MM-DD`, or `undefined` if the API returns non-OK, the file has no commits, or the request fails.

## TypeScript types

### Exported from `vike-sitemap-generator`

```ts
// Main plugin
function vikeSitemap(options: SitemapPluginOptions): Plugin;

// Fetchers
function getLastModFromGit(options: { filePath: string; cwd?: string }): Promise<string | undefined>;
function getLastModFromGithub(options: { token: string; repo: string; filePath: string }): Promise<string | undefined>;

// Types
interface SitemapPluginOptions {
  baseUrl: string;
  outFile?: string;
  outDir?: string;
  trailingSlash?: TrailingSlashConfig;
  lastmod?: (url: string) => Promise<string | undefined> | string | undefined;
  priority?: PriorityConfig;
  changefreq?: ChangefreqConfig;
  images?: (url: string) => Promise<SitemapImage[] | undefined> | SitemapImage[] | undefined;
  additionalUrls?: string[];
  exclude?: (string | RegExp)[];
  concurrency?: number;
  maxUrlsPerSitemap?: number;
  robots?: boolean;
  dryRun?: boolean;
  externalSitemaps?: string[];
}

interface SitemapPageConfig {
  priority?: number;
  changefreq?: Changefreq;
  lastmod?: string;
  images?: SitemapImage[];
  exclude?: boolean;
}

interface SitemapPageContext<Data = unknown> {
  url: string;
  routeParams: Record<string, string>;
  data: Data;
}

type SitemapPageConfigFn<Data = unknown> = (context: SitemapPageContext<Data>) => SitemapPageConfig | Promise<SitemapPageConfig>;

type SitemapUrlsConfig = string[] | (() => string[] | Promise<string[]>);

interface SitemapImage {
  loc: string;
  caption?: string;
  geoLocation?: string;
  title?: string;
  license?: string;
}

interface SitemapEntry {
  loc: string;
  lastmod?: string;
  priority?: number;
  changefreq?: Changefreq;
  images?: SitemapImage[];
}

interface SitemapContext {
  urls: string[];
}

interface CollectedUrl {
  url: string;
  pageConfig?: SitemapPageConfig | SitemapPageConfigFn;
  routeParams?: Record<string, string>;
  data?: any;
}

type Changefreq = "always" | "hourly" | "daily" | "weekly" | "monthly" | "yearly" | "never";

type PriorityConfig = number | PriorityRule[] | PriorityFn;
type PriorityFn = (url: string, context: SitemapContext) => number | undefined;
interface PriorityRule { match: string | RegExp; priority: number; }

type ChangefreqConfig = Changefreq | ChangefreqRule[];
interface ChangefreqRule { match: string | RegExp; changefreq: Changefreq; }

type TrailingSlashConfig = boolean | TrailingSlashRule[] | TrailingSlashFn;
type TrailingSlashFn = (url: string, context: SitemapContext) => boolean;
interface TrailingSlashRule { match: string | RegExp; trailingSlash: boolean; }
```

### Exported from `vike-sitemap-generator/config`

```ts
// Default export: Vike extension config
export default {
  name: "vike-sitemap-generator",
  meta: {
    sitemap: {
      env: { config: true, server: true },
    },
    sitemapUrls: {
      env: { config: true, server: true },
    },
  },
} satisfies Config;

// Augments Vike.Config globally:
declare global {
  namespace Vike {
    interface Config {
      sitemap?: SitemapPageConfig | SitemapPageConfigFn;
      sitemapUrls?: SitemapUrlsConfig;
    }
  }
}
```

## How it works

The plugin hooks into Vite's build pipeline and runs after the SSR build completes. It waits for the SSR build so that `prerenderContext.pageContexts` (populated during the SSR build's `writeBundle`) and page configs loaded across Vite environment boundaries are available.

1. `configResolved` -- captures the Vite resolved config.
2. `closeBundle` (SSR build only):
   - Re-reads Vike's page configuration via `getVikeConfig()` to pick up `prerenderContext` and per-page `+sitemap.ts` / `+sitemapUrls.ts` metadata
   - Collects URLs from `prerenderContext` (SSG) or static route patterns (SSR), plus URLs enumerated by `+sitemapUrls.ts` files
   - Attaches per-page `+sitemap.ts` config to each URL
   - Filters out excluded paths (global `exclude` patterns and per-page `exclude: true`)
   - Applies trailing slash rules
   - Resolves metadata (`lastmod`, `priority`, `changefreq`, `images`) in parallel with the configured concurrency, merging per-page values over global callbacks
   - Resolves image asset imports via the client build manifest
   - Splits into multiple sitemap files if the URL count exceeds `maxUrlsPerSitemap`
   - Writes files to the resolved output directory (or logs in dry-run mode)
   - Appends a `Sitemap:` directive to `robots.txt` if enabled

## Options summary table

| Option              | Type                                          | Default        | Description                                                       |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `baseUrl`           | `string`                                      | (required)     | Base URL (e.g. `"https://example.com"`)                           |
| `outFile`           | `string`                                      | `"sitemap.xml"`| Output filename relative to outDir                                |
| `outDir`            | `string`                                      | Vite's outDir  | Independent output directory                                      |
| `trailingSlash`     | `boolean \| TrailingSlashRule[] \| function`   | `undefined`    | Trailing slash configuration                                      |
| `lastmod`           | `(url: string) => string \| undefined`        | `undefined`    | Async callback for `<lastmod>`                                    |
| `priority`          | `number \| PriorityRule[] \| function`         | `undefined`    | Priority configuration                                            |
| `changefreq`        | `Changefreq \| ChangefreqRule[]`               | `undefined`    | Change frequency configuration                                    |
| `images`            | `(url: string) => SitemapImage[] \| undefined` | `undefined`    | Async callback for `<image:image>`                                |
| `additionalUrls`    | `string[]`                                    | `[]`           | Extra URLs to include                                             |
| `exclude`           | `(string \| RegExp)[]`                         | `[]`           | URL patterns to exclude                                           |
| `concurrency`       | `number`                                      | `10`           | Max concurrent metadata resolutions                               |
| `maxUrlsPerSitemap` | `number`                                      | `50000`        | Threshold for sitemap index splitting                             |
| `robots`            | `boolean`                                     | `false`        | Append `Sitemap:` directive to robots.txt                         |
| `dryRun`            | `boolean`                                     | `false`        | Log XML to console without writing files                          |
| `externalSitemaps`  | `string[]`                                    | `[]`           | External sitemap URLs to include in the sitemap index             |
